titleStmt.title: SII 17.727: original edition by K. G. Krishnan
No. 727. (A.R. No. 670 of 1904.) TIRUMULLAIVĀYAL, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the west wall of the central shrine in the Māśilāmaṇīśvara temple.
The provenance of this record and its palaeography suggest the identity of the king with Sundarapāṇḍya I.

This inscription records an agreement among the inhabitants of Puḻaṟ-kōṭṭam alias Vikramaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, regarding a gift of 148 māḍai, by Śendāmaraikkaṇṇan of Tirumullaivāyal, in Kāṉappēṟūr-nāḍu, for conducting a special service called Vikkiramaśōḻa-vaḷanāṭṭār Sandi, the Vaikāśi festivals and for repairs in the temple of Tirumullaivāyil-uḍaiya nāyaṉār.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
koṟcaṭapanmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīcuntarapāṇṭiyatevarkku yāṇṭu 10 8 vatu jayaṅ koṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu puḻaṟ koṭṭa
māṉa vikkiramacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu nāṭṭārom vivastai Iṉṉāṭṭu Uṭaiyār tirumullai vāyiluṭaiya nāyaṉārkkup pūcaikkum tiruppaṇikkum nāṭṭup pe
rāl vikkiramacoḻa vaḷanāṭṭār canti Amutu ceytaruḷavum tiruvaikācit tirunāḷ Eḻuntaruḷu vikkavum Innāyaṉār tevatāṉam tirunā
mattuk kāṇiyāṉa kāṉappeṟūr nāṭṭut tirumullaivāyal centāmaraikkaṇṇan tarappaṭi māṭai ṉūṟṟuṉāṟpattu Eṭṭukku nāṭṭār viva
staippaṭi paṇattil Iṟukka niccayitta Oṉṟu pāti paṇa nikki nikkip pūcaikkum tiruppaṇik kum tirunāḷukkum kaḻitta Oṉṟu pāti
Oṉṟu pātiyāl vanta paṇam nāṭṭile Iṟukkak kaṭavomākavum payir tiṭṭik kaṭamai koḷḷum Āṇṭukku tiṭṭiṉa payirukku pattil
Aiñcu nikki nikki niṉṟa mutalil Oṉṟu pātiyāl varu mutal nāṭṭile Eṟṟi Iṟukkak kaṭavom Ippaṭikku cantirātittavarai celvatāka cempi
lum kallilum veṭṭikkoḷḷac cammatittom puḻaṟ koṭṭamāṉa vikkiramacoḻa vaḷa nāṭṭārom Ivai cittirameḻiveḷāṉ Eḻuttu Itu paṉmāheśvararakṣai .
Digital edition of SII 17.727 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
